summary: Matched stage function grInishie1_801FBC4C by introducing a local HSD_JObj* variable for gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2 before passing it to DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP. The PR body says this fixed register allocation and brought the function to 100% match; no behavioral change is evident from the diff.

key_files.role: Only changed file; adjusted grInishie1_801FBC4C to use a local HSD_JObj* before DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP.

what_changed: In grInishie1_801FBC4C, replaced direct use of gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2 inside DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP with a local variable: HSD_JObj* jobj2.
DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP now receives jobj2 instead of the full nested struct expression.
Existing cleanup of gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].hatena_gobj via Ground_801C4A08 and assignment to NULL was left unchanged.
Net code change was very small: 2 added lines and 1 deleted line in src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c.
smart_moves: Used a typed local pointer to steer compiler register allocation without changing function behavior.
Kept the matching fix narrowly scoped to the expression that affected codegen.
Preserved the surrounding cleanup sequence, reducing risk while targeting only the mismatch.
decomp_lessons: For stubborn one-function mismatches, splitting a nested struct field access into a local variable can be enough to change register allocation and produce a match.
Macros such as DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP may be sensitive to whether their argument is a complex lvalue expression or a simple local pointer.
When matching stage code, local temporaries around GET_GROUND-derived fields can be legitimate codegen tools, not just readability changes.
Small, isolated PRs are useful for validating decomp matching tactics with low review burden.
naming_conventions: The local variable was named jobj2 to mirror the existing struct field gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index].jobj2.
The type HSD_JObj* was used explicitly for the local pointer passed to DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP.
Existing project-style names were preserved: grInishie1_801FBC4C, Ground_801C4A08, hatena_gobj, GET_GROUND.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce a local variable for a nested field access to influence register allocation.
Prefer a simple local pointer argument to a macro when the direct nested expression causes a mismatch.
Use minimal source restructuring when the intended behavior is already correct but codegen differs.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
Author comment indicated this was a small first PR to test the waters and asked reviewers to point out anything missed.
follow_up_queries: Find other DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP mismatches where nested struct field access should be split into a local HSD_JObj*.
Search grInishie1.c for remaining unmatched functions using gp->gv.inishie1.blocks[index] fields directly in macros.
Compare similar stage cleanup functions that clear jobj2 and hatena_gobj for consistent local variable patterns.
